name: samgis-core
documentation:
# SamGIS CORE

Core functionality for [SamGIS](https://github.com/trincadev/samgis-be) project:
- instance segmentation and recognition with a [segment anything](https://segment-anything.com/) model
- various utilities (logging, serialize, type hints, etcetera)

modules.name: samgis_core
documentation: Get machine learning predictions from geodata raster images

modules.name: samgis_core.utilities.constants
documentation: Project constants

modules.name: samgis_core.utilities
documentation: various helpers functions

functions.name: samgis_core.utilities.frontend_builder.assert_envs
documentation:
Assert env variables are not empty.

Args:
    envs_list: list of env variables

Returns:

functions.name: samgis_core.utilities.frontend_builder.read_std_out_err
documentation:
Capture the standard output or standard error content for a given system command pipe.

Args:
    std_out_err: str
    output_type: output type (stdout or stderr)
    command: command executed

Returns:

functions.name: samgis_core.utilities.frontend_builder.run_command
documentation:
Run a system command and capture its output.
See https://docs.python.org/3.11/library/subprocess.html#subprocess.run for more details

Args:
    commands_list: list of single string commands
    capture_output: if true, stdout and stderr will be captured
    text: if true, capture stdout and stderr as strings
    check: If check is true, and the process exits with a non-zero exit code, a CalledProcessError exception will
           be raised. Attributes of that exception hold the arguments, the exit code, and stdout and stderr if they
           were captured.
    env: environment variables

Returns:

functions.name: samgis_core.utilities.frontend_builder.build_frontend
documentation:
Build a static [Vue js](https://vuejs.org/), [tailwindcss](https://tailwindcss.com/) frontend.
If force_build is False, the function also check if index_page_filename and output_css_filename already exists:
in this case skip the build.

Args:
    project_root_folder: Project folder that contains the static frontend
    input_css_path: file path pointing to the input css file
    output_dist_folder: dist folder path where to write the frontend bundle
    index_page_filename: index html filename
    output_css_filename: output css filename
    force_build: useful to skip the frontend build

Returns:
    state of the build (True in case of build completed, False in case of build skipped)

functions.name: samgis_core.utilities.frontend_builder.get_installed_node
documentation:
Get the installed node bin folder given the environment variables:
- NODE_DIR (current node installed), without the bin subfolder
- NODE_DIR_PARENT (parent folder of the node installed versions, e.g. `~/.nvm/versions/node/` )

Returns:
    node bin path

functions.name: samgis_core.utilities.frontend_builder.get_path_with_node_dir
documentation:
Get the PATH environment variable with the node bin folder.

Returns:
    PATH environment variable with the node bin folder

modules.name: samgis_core.utilities.utilities
documentation: Various utilities (logger, time benchmark, args dump, numerical and stats info)

functions.name: samgis_core.utilities.utilities.base64_decode
documentation:
Decode base64 strings

Args:
    s: input string

Returns:
    decoded string

functions.name: samgis_core.utilities.utilities.base64_encode
documentation:
Encode input strings or bytes as base64

Args:
    sb: input string or bytes

Returns:
    base64 encoded bytes

functions.name: samgis_core.utilities.utilities.hash_calculate
documentation:
Return computed hash from input variable (typically a numpy array).

Args:
    arr_or_path: input variable
    is_file: whether input is a file or not
    read_mode: mode for reading file

Returns:
    computed hash from input variable

functions.name: samgis_core.utilities.utilities.convert_ndarray_to_pil
documentation:
Check if an image is a ndarray and then convert to a PIL Image instance.

Args:
    pil_image: PIL image or ndarray

Returns:
    PIL Image

functions.name: samgis_core.utilities.utilities.apply_coords
documentation:
Expects a numpy np_array of length 2 in the final dimension. Requires the
original image size in (H, W) format.

    Args:
        coords: coordinates ndarray
        embedding: PIL image embedding dict

    Returns:
        coordinates ndarray

functions.name: samgis_core.utilities.utilities.normalize_array
documentation:
Normalize numpy array between 0 and 'new_h' value. Default dtype of output array is int


Args:
    arr: input numpy array
    new_h: max value of output array
    type_normalization: default dtype of output array

Returns:
    numpy array

modules.name: samgis_core.utilities.type_hints
documentation: custom type hints

functions.name: samgis_core.utilities.create_folders_if_not_exists.create_folder_if_not_exists
documentation:
Create a folder given its path.

Args:
    pathname: folder Path or string

Returns:

functions.name: samgis_core.utilities.create_folders_if_not_exists.folders_creation
documentation:
Create all folders listed within the folders_map argument (this argument can be a dict or a json string).
If folders_map is None the function will try to load the 'FOLDERS_MAP' env variable, then will load that json into
dict. Once loaded and parsed the folders_map variable, the function will loop over the dict to create the folders
using the `create_folder_if_not_exists()` function.

Args:
    folders_map: dict or string map of folder string
    ignore_errors: bool needed to eventually ignore errors on folder creation

Returns:

functions.name: samgis_core.utilities.session_logger.rename_event_key
documentation:
Log entries keep the text message in the `event` field, but Datadog
uses the `message` field. This processor moves the value from one field to
the other.
See https://github.com/hynek/structlog/issues/35#issuecomment-591321744
https://github.com/hynek/structlog/issues/35#issuecomment-591321744

functions.name: samgis_core.utilities.session_logger.drop_color_message_key
documentation:
Uvicorn logs the message a second time in the extra `color_message`, but we don't
need it. This processor drops the key from the event dict if it exists.

functions.name: samgis_core.utilities.session_logger.setup_logging
documentation:
Enhance the configuration of structlog.
Needed for correlation id injection with fastapi middleware in samgis-web.
After the use of logging_middleware() in samgis_web.web.middlewares, add also the CorrelationIdMiddleware from
'asgi_correlation_id' package. (See 'tests/web/test_middlewares.py' in samgis_web).
To change an input parameter like the log level, re-run the function changing the parameter
(no need to re-instantiate the logger instance: it's a hot change)

Args:
    json_logs: set logs in json format
    log_level: log level string

Returns:

functions.name: samgis_core.utilities.plot_images.helper_imshow_output_expected
documentation:
Simple way to display a list of images with their titles, color map.
Should work also in an automate environments, like tests (use a `close_after` argument > 0)

Args:
    img_list: ndarray images to display
    titles_list: title images
    cmap: color map
    plot_size: figure plot size
    show: fire plt.show() action if needed
    debug: workaround useful in an interactive context, like Pycharm debugger
    close_after: close after give seconds (useful in tests, contrasted to 'debug' option)

Returns:
    tuple of matplotlib Figure, Axes

functions.name: samgis_core.utilities.plot_images.imshow_raster
documentation:
Displays raster images lists/arrays with titles, legend, alpha transparency, figure sizes
and geographic transformations, if not none (leveraging rasterio.plot)

Args:
    raster: image to display
    title: title image
    cmap: color map
    interpolation: interpolation type
    alpha: alpha transparency
    transform: geographic transform, eventually used for map representation by rasterio
    plot_size: figure plot size
    show: fire plt.show() action if needed
    debug: workaround useful in an interactive context, like Pycharm debugger
    close_after: close after give seconds (useful in tests, contrasted to 'debug' option)
    backend: matplotlib backend string

Returns:
    tuple of matplotlib Figure, Axes

modules.name: samgis_core.utilities.serialize
documentation: Serialize objects

functions.name: samgis_core.utilities.serialize.serialize
documentation:
Return the input object into a serializable one

Args:
    obj: Object to serialize
    include_none: bool to indicate if include also keys with None values during dict serialization

Returns:
    serialized object

modules.name: samgis_core.prediction_api
documentation: functions useful to handle machine learning models

functions.name: samgis_core.utilities.update_requirements_txt.get_dependencies_freeze
documentation: get a 'freeze.txt'-like dict of 'name':'version' metadata about installed packages.

functions.name: samgis_core.utilities.update_requirements_txt.get_requirements_txt
documentation:
Write on disk a requirements.txt file with an updated list of dependencies from installed python packages.
Both input and output requirements files must be within the folder from which the current command is executed.

Args:
    requirements_no_versions_filename: input requirements filename with no specified versions
    requirements_output_filename: output requirements.txt filename

Returns:

functions.name: samgis_core.prediction_api.sam_onnx_inference.get_raster_inference
documentation:
Get inference output for a given image using a SegmentAnythingONNX model

Args:
    img: input PIL Image
    prompt: list of prompt dict
    models_instance: SegmentAnythingONNX instance model
    model_name: model name string

Returns:
    raster prediction mask, prediction number

functions.name: samgis_core.prediction_api.sam_onnx_inference.get_inference_embedding
documentation:
add an embedding to the embedding dict if needed

Args:
    img: input PIL Image
    models_instance: SegmentAnythingONNX instance model
    model_name: model name string
    embedding_key: embedding id
    embedding_dict: embedding dict object

Returns:
    raster dict

functions.name: samgis_core.prediction_api.sam_onnx_inference.get_raster_inference_using_existing_embedding
documentation:
Get inference output for a given image using a SegmentAnythingONNX model, using an existing embedding instead of a
new ndarray or PIL image

Args:
    embedding: dict
    prompt: list of prompt dict
    models_instance: SegmentAnythingONNX instance model
    folder_write_tmp_on_disk: output folder where to write debug images
    key: embedding key

Returns:
    raster prediction mask, prediction number

functions.name: samgis_core.prediction_api.sam_onnx_inference.get_raster_inference_with_embedding_from_dict
documentation:
Get inference output using a SegmentAnythingONNX model, but get the image embedding from the given embedding dict
 instead of creating a new embedding. This function needs the img argument to update the embedding dict if necessary

Args:
    img: input PIL Image
    prompt: list of prompt dict
    models_instance: SegmentAnythingONNX instance model
    model_name: model name string
    embedding_key: embedding id
    embedding_dict: embedding images dict
    folder_write_tmp_on_disk: output folder where to write debug images

Returns:
    raster prediction mask, prediction number

modules.name: samgis_core.prediction_api.sam_onnx2
documentation:

classes.name: samgis_core.prediction_api.sam_onnx2.SegmentAnythingONNX2

methods.name: samgis_core.prediction_api.sam_onnx2.SegmentAnythingONNX2.get_input_points
documentation:
Get input points from a prompt dict list.

Args:
    prompt: dict list

Returns:
    tuple of points, labels ndarray ready for Segment Anything inference

methods.name: samgis_core.prediction_api.sam_onnx2.SegmentAnythingONNX2.encode
documentation:
Calculate embedding and metadata for a single image.

Args:
    img: input image to embed

Returns:
    embedding image dict useful to store and cache image embeddings

methods.name: samgis_core.prediction_api.sam_onnx2.SegmentAnythingONNX2.predict_masks
documentation:
Predict masks for a single image.

Args:
    embedding: input image embedding dict
    prompt: Segment Anything input prompt

Returns:
    prediction masks ndarray; this should have (1, 1, **image.shape) shape

methods.name: samgis_core.prediction_api.sam_onnx2.SegmentAnythingONNX2.preprocess_image
documentation:
Resize image preserving aspect ratio using `output_size_target` as a long side.

Args:
    img: input ndarray/PIL image

Returns:
    image ndarray

methods.name: samgis_core.prediction_api.sam_onnx2.SegmentAnythingONNX2.padding_tensor
documentation:
Pad an image ndarray/tensor to given instance self.target_size

Args:
    img: input ndarray/PIL image

Returns:
    image ndarray

Segmentation model using Segment Anything.
Compatible with onnxruntime 1.17.x and later
